title: Lesson 189: (Grammar) - the expectation is that ...; the reason is that .....

intro_cards.title: Explanation/Emphasis: のです
intro_cards.body:
のです (often のだ in plain speech) signals explanation or seeking explanation.

• 頭が痛いんです。(The thing is, my head hurts.)
• どうしたんですか？(What happened? / What's the matter?)
• 明日、試験があるんです。
(The thing is, I have an exam tomorrow — giving context)

のです adds nuance of:
• Giving a reason/explanation
• Sharing context the listener needs
• Softening a statement or question

のです (formal) ≈ んです (casual contraction)

In questions, んですか invites the other person to explain something you noticed.
